summary: Chapter 2 drafted successfully from Lirien's POV (5,935 words). Three scenes completed: (1) Morning surveillance reveals Kaelith is compressed not broken, operative-grade threat awareness contradicts intelligence file. (2) Direct eye contact confrontation in market square — Kaelith asks if Lirien is here to kill her, Lirien deflects with precise non-denial, both recognize each other as trained operatives. (3) Seep intelligence packet reveals operational paradox: Oathbrand flared simultaneously with Pallor tear, suggesting Kaelith has Warden-lineage capacity; executing her may destroy access to god-seal maintenance. Lirien decides to delay execution to investigate, creating 48-hour binding deadline. Voice fingerprint maintained — Lirien's analytical precision, water/infiltration metaphors, observation→assessment sentence structure distinct from Kaelith's percussive brevity. Dramatic irony sustained: reader knows kill contract, Kaelith suspects but doesn't know. Fantasy and romance arcs interleaved: Warden revelation makes assassination strategically catastrophic, not just morally complex. All craft tools applied per metadata. Continuity maintained with ch1 events.

notes: Chapter 2 establishes Lirien as POV character with fully distinct voice. Operational paradox (Warden inheritance discovery) transforms simple assassination into world-scale crisis management. 48-hour binding deadline creates urgency for ch3-4. Direct confrontation scene provides reader with dramatic irony payoff (we know the threat, Kaelith suspects it, Lirien deflects precisely). Water/infiltration motifs seeded. Romance beat: Lirien's first admission (internal only) that Kaelith is 'interesting' beyond target parameters — first vulnerability step. All invariants maintained: no info-dumping (worldbuilding through intelligence packet is character-appropriate), show-don't-tell (emotions through classification system failure), dialogue naturalism (subtext-heavy exchange), POV distinctness within opening paragraph.
